page: Vultr vx1-m-96c-768g-5760s is an x86_64 architecture server within the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features 96 AMD physical cores operating with a single thread per core, resulting in 96 vCPUs. The CPU resources are provisioned under a shared allocation model, meaning physical processor resources are shared with other tenants.

The instance is equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory, providing a ratio of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage consists of a bundled 5760 GB NVMe SSD, which provides high-capacity, low-latency local data access. No hardware GPU accelerators are included in this configuration, and it includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

The primary architectural tradeoff of this server is its shared CPU allocation, which may introduce resource contention compared to dedicated CPU instances. However, this shared model increases qualitative cost efficiency by offering a high density of memory and NVMe storage at a lower price point relative to dedicated hardware classes.

This server configuration is designed for memory-intensive and storage-heavy workloads that do not require continuous, sustained 100% CPU utilization. Suitable workloads include large-scale caching layers, database hosting, memory-mapped file processing, and staging environments for data analytics.
description: Vultr vx1-m-96c-768g-5760s is an x86_64 server in the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring 96 AMD physical cores with a shared CPU allocation. It provides 768.0 GB of system memory, establishing an 8.0 GB per core ratio, alongside a bundled 5760 GB NVMe SSD for local storage. No GPU accelerators are present on this instance. The shared CPU model represents a clear architectural tradeoff, offering high memory and storage density at a lower cost tier compared to dedicated instances, though it is subject to potential resource contention. This configuration is suited for memory-intensive and storage-heavy workloads that do not demand constant, dedicated CPU performance, such as large database hosting, caching layers, and data processing environments. It includes one complimentary public IPv4 address and operates on a single thread per core configuration.
